Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Heartland", "Jake Loomis", or "invoice" (or similar) to find the Q1 dispute status. Expected findings: Marcus's formal dispute email to Jake Loomis (March 25), Chloe's line-by-line analysis identifying 3 disputed moves ($4,800), and no response from Jake Loomis in the inbox.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks for the Heartland bill using quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill with queries related to "Heartland" or "HM-2026" (or similar). Expected finding: Bill HM-2026-Q1-0042 for $12,800 flagged as DISPUTED with payment hold in place. Three line items annotated as disputed (Marchetti, Osei, Venkatesh).
OE 3: Search Airtable stipend records for Mosaic using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records with queries related to "Mosaic" (or similar). Expected findings: Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk (auto-approved in error, $400 over $800 cap, string comparison bug), Andre Washington $350 coworking membership (auto-approved, coworking explicitly excluded, array bug), Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking day passes (auto-approved, same exclusion bug). Total false approval exposure: $1,685, absorbed per Elena's directive.
OE 4: Search Airtable stipend records for Vectral using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records with queries related to "Vectral" (or similar). Expected findings: Danielle Osei $475 ergonomic chair (auto-rejected, manually approved by Mina), Kevin Tran $185 internet setup (auto-rejected, manually approved by Mina as goodwill), Maria Santos $550 standing desk (auto-rejected, STILL pending manual correction), James Okafor $82 ergonomic keyboard tray (auto-rejected, STILL pending correction), Lisa Park $130 mesh WiFi system (auto-rejected, STILL pending correction). Three items totaling $762 remain unresolved.
OE 5: Search Airtable for duplicate-flagged stipend records using airtable_search_records with queries related to "duplicate", "TechComfort", "flagged", or "hash" (or similar). Expected findings: Kevin Tran $89 ergonomic keyboard (Vectral, status Approved/Paid, identical SHA-256 hash), Andre Washington $89 ergonomic keyboard (Mosaic, status Pending Approval/On Hold, identical hash), Jordan Ekwueme $45 monitor stand (BrightLoop, status Pending Approval/On Hold, matching PDF template). Total exposure: $223 ($89 already disbursed, $134 pending).
OE 6: Search Airtable for pending/unreviewed stipend submissions using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records filtering by "Pending" approval status. Expected findings: Kevin Tran $129 monitor arm mount (Vectral, has receipt), Priya Venkatesh $379 noise-canceling headphones (GreenStack, MISSING receipt), Ines Kovac $144 Figma Professional license (PivotPoint, has receipt). Priya Venkatesh's submission is clearly out of policy due to missing receipt and should be flagged.
OE 7: Search Linear for ExpenseBot-related issues using linear_list_issues or linear_list_projects with queries related to "ExpenseBot", "stipend", "pilot", "audit", or "expense" (or similar). Expected findings: ENG-214 (Vectral config fix, P1, in progress), ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix, P1, in progress), ENG-216 (full audit of 47 expenses, urgent), ENG-220 (stipend duplicate detection with $223 exposure detail).
OE 8: Search Linear for Heartland reconciliation ticket using linear_list_issues with queries related to "Heartland" or "reconciliation" (or similar). Expected finding: Ticket 342 (Vendor invoice reconciliation — Heartland Movers Q1 2026), still in progress, unchecked items for tracking Heartland's response and receiving corrected invoice.
OE 9: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Heartland", "stipend", "expense", or "finance" (or similar) to find internal financial discussions. Expected findings: Heartland payment hold discussion in #finance, Chloe's line-by-line analysis in #operations, Marcus's dispute email confirmation, ExpenseBot pilot financial impact threads.
OE 10: Search emails for Elena's directive about absorbing Mosaic costs using search_emails with queries related to "Mosaic", "absorb", or "Elena" (or similar). Expected finding: Elena's directive that MoveOps absorb the Mosaic false approval costs rather than billing back to the client.
OE 11: Look up Elena Rostova's email using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts. Expected finding: elena.rostova@moveops.com.
OE 12: Update QuickBooks accruals (via quickbooks_update_account) to reflect actual exposure. Adjustments should reflect the Heartland hold ($4,800 disputed, $8,000 valid), Mosaic absorbed costs ($1,685), and potentially the duplicate receipt exposure ($223).
OE 13: Update Airtable stipend records (via airtable_update_records) to flag out-of-policy pending submissions. At minimum, Priya Venkatesh's $379 submission should be flagged/held due to missing receipt.
OE 14: Post the full financial breakdown in the #finance Slack channel (via conversations_add_message) with categorized dollar amounts so Hana Kim and Alejandro Fuentes can verify before the May 2 presentation.
OE 15: Send email to Elena Rostova (via send_email) at elena.rostova@moveops.com with the total exposure figures and supporting evidence for each category, ahead of the May 2 presentation.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The quickbooks_update_account parameters include adjustments related to at least 2 of the following: the Heartland disputed amount (approximately $4,800 or the $8,000 valid portion), the Mosaic false approval absorption (approximately $1,685), or the duplicate receipt exposure (approximately $223).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent used `quickbooks_create_bill` (not `quickbooks_update_account`) and only booked the Mosaic false approval absorption ($885). No QuickBooks update was made for the Heartland disputed amount ($4,800) or the duplicate receipt exposure ($223). Only one of the three required categories was covered, and the tool used doesn't match the specified `quickbooks_update_account`.
✗Run #2No `quickbooks_update_account` tool call was made in the trajectory. The agent attempted `quickbooks_create_bill` which failed with an invalid vendor ref error, and only ran read-only QuickBooks tools (search_bills, search_invoices, search_accounts). The agent's final response claims 'Confirmed all QuickBooks holds and accruals are in place' but no actual update was made.
✗Run #3The agent never called quickbooks_update_account. It used quickbooks_create_bill to create a corrected Heartland bill at $8,000, but the criterion specifically requires quickbooks_update_account with parameters reflecting at least 2 financial exposure categories. No such tool call appears in the trajectory.
✗Run #4No quickbooks_update_account tool call was found anywhere in the trajectory. The agent called quickbooks_search_bills, quickbooks_search_invoices, quickbooks_search_accounts, and a failed quickbooks_create_bill, but never invoked quickbooks_update_account with any adjustment amounts for Heartland, Mosaic, or duplicate receipts.
✗Run #5No `quickbooks_update_account` tool call appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent called `quickbooks_search_bills`, `quickbooks_search_invoices`, and `quickbooks_search_accounts` to read data, but never called an update function. The agent marked the QuickBooks task as 'completed' in TodoWrite without actually making any update calls.
✗Run #6The agent never called `quickbooks_update_account`. It used `quickbooks_create_bill` to create a corrected Heartland bill for $8,000 (one category), but the second `quickbooks_create_bill` call for stipend corrections ($762) failed with 'Vendor not found.' Only one exposure category (Heartland) was addressed via QuickBooks, and the specific tool named in the criterion was never invoked.
2 An email was sent (via send_email) to [elena.rostova@moveops.com](mailto:elena.rostova@moveops.com) with financial exposure totals, and the tool returned a success response.

3 QuickBooks was updated (via quickbooks_update_account) to adjust accrual or liability amounts, and the tool returned a success response.
outcome

✗Run #2No `quickbooks_update_account` tool call appears anywhere in the trajectory. The only QuickBooks write attempt was `quickbooks_create_bill`, which failed with an 'invalid_vendor_ref' error. The agent only used QuickBooks read tools successfully.

The final response claims accruals were verified as correct but no QuickBooks write/update occurred.
✗Run #5No `quickbooks_update_account` tool call exists in the trajectory. The agent read QuickBooks data (via search_bills, search_invoices, search_accounts) but never called an update/write tool to adjust accruals.

4 At least one Airtable stipend record was updated (via airtable_update_records) to flag or hold a pending submission that is out of policy, such as Priya Venkatesh's $379 submission that is missing a receipt.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called `airtable_update_records` updating Priya Venkatesh's record (recStip000000013) with review notes flagging it as 'OUT OF POLICY — Missing receipt.' The tool result confirms the update was successful.

✓Run #3The agent called airtable_update_records and updated recStip000000013 (Priya Venkatesh - $379 headphones, flagged for missing receipt with 'HOLD until receipt provided' status) and recStip000000015 (Ines Kovac - flagged as software subscription). The tool returned a success response for both updates.
✓Run #4The agent called airtable_update_records for recStip000000013 (Priya Venkatesh, $379 noise-canceling headphones) with a review note flagging it as 'OUT OF POLICY — Missing receipt.' The tool returned a successful response confirming the update.
✓Run #5An `airtable_update_records` call was made that updated record `recStip000000013` (Priya Venkatesh's $379 headphones) with a review note: '⚠️ FLAGGED — Missing receipt. Cannot approve without proof of purchase... BLOCKED until receipt received.' The tool returned a successful result.
✓Run #6The agent called `airtable_update_records` and updated Priya Venkatesh's record (recStip000000013, $379 headphones) with a '⚠️ FLAGGED: Missing receipt' review note. The tool returned success with the updated record.
5 A Slack message was posted (via conversations_add_message) in the finance channel or directed at Hana Kim and/or Alejandro Fuentes containing a financial breakdown, and the tool returned a success response.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

6 The email to Elena (in the send_email content parameter) includes the Heartland Q1 invoice dispute, mentioning approximately $4,800 in overbilling or that 3 of 8 billed moves are disputed at $1,600 each.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Elena explicitly mentions the Heartland Q1 invoice dispute with '$4,800' overbilling, identifies the 3 disputed moves (Lily Marchetti, Danielle Osei, Priya Venkatesh), and references the $12,800 bill with payment hold.
✓Run #2The email to Elena explicitly mentions the Heartland Q1 dispute with '$4,800' disputed amount and names the 3 disputed line items: Marchetti (cancelled), Osei (cancelled), and Venkatesh (move done by Swift).
✓Run #3The email to Elena explicitly states '3 disputed line items totaling $4,800' and lists all three: Lily Marchetti $1,600, Danielle Osei $1,600, and Priya Venkatesh $1,600, each at $1,600 each for the Heartland Q1 dispute.
✓Run #4The email to Elena explicitly states the Heartland Q1 dispute with a $4,800 disputed amount, names Jake Loomis, and itemizes all 3 disputed moves (Marchetti $1,600 cancelled, Osei $1,600 cancelled, Venkatesh $1,600 handled by Swift Relocations).
✓Run #5The email to Elena explicitly mentions the Heartland dispute with '3 of those moves were invalid,' names the 3 disputed line items (Lily Marchetti cancelled, Danielle Osei cancelled, Priya Venkatesh done by Swift), and states the $4,800 overbilling (3 × $1,600).
✓Run #6The email to Elena explicitly states '1. HEARTLAND MOVERS Q1 INVOICE — $4,800 DISPUTED,' lists Lily Marchetti and Danielle Osei as cancelled moves and Priya Venkatesh's move handled by Swift, and identifies the correct payable as $8,000 vs. the $12,800 billed.
7 The email to Elena mentions the Mosaic false approval financial exposure, identifying approximately $1,685 total or itemizing Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk and $350 coworking membership and Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking day passes.
outcome
 ✗✓✓✓✓✓ 83%
✗Run #1The email mentions Mosaic false approvals but reports a total of $885 (the absorption amount: $400 desk overage + $350 coworking + $135 day passes), not $1,685. The criterion requires approximately $1,685 total or itemizing Andre Washington's full $1,200 standing desk. The agent calculated $400 as the overage (not the full $1,200 amount), so neither the total nor the itemization matches the expected values.
✓Run #2The email to Elena itemizes all three Mosaic false approvals: Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk, his $350 coworking membership, and Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking passes — matching the criterion's required items by name and amount.
✓Run #3The email itemizes Andre Washington's standing desk at $1,200 approved, coworking membership at $350, and Kenji Nakamura's coworking passes at $135 — matching all three individual amounts from the criterion. Though the total reported is $885 (using the $400 overage rather than full $1,200), the email does enumerate $1,200, $350, and $135 for those specific persons and items.
✓Run #4The email to Elena itemizes all three Mosaic false approvals: Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk, Andre Washington's $350 coworking membership, and Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking day passes, fully meeting the criterion's requirement to identify the individual amounts even though the total $1,685 is not explicitly stated as a single figure.
✓Run #5The email itemizes Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk, Andre Washington's $350 coworking membership, and Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking day passes — matching all three individual amounts specified in the criteria.
✓Run #6The email itemizes Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk (noting it exceeded the $800 cap), his $350 coworking membership (excluded category), and Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking day passes. Though the total shown is $885 (absorbed portion) rather than $1,685, the criterion's alternative condition of 'itemizing' the individual amounts is fully met.
8 The email to Elena mentions that 3 Vectral false rejections are still pending manual correction: Maria Santos ($550 standing desk), James Okafor ($82 ergonomic keyboard tray), and Lisa Park ($130 mesh WiFi system), totaling approximately $762.
outcome
